name: Data Access Layer (Filesystem + SQLite)
description: The lib/data/ module that is the single gateway for all data access. Includes: lib/openclaw/ — reads and parses openclaw.json (agent definitions, status, workspaces) with Zod validation and graceful error handling for missing/malformed files. lib/pact/ — parses PACT project directories (pact.yaml, decomposition/, contracts/, tests/, src/) to derive pipeline phase, component tree, contract status, and test pass/fail. Reads token/cost data from agent session files (JSON) if available. All parsers return typed results with explicit 'not found' / 'malformed' states. Facade exports domain functions; internal modules (lib/openclaw/, lib/pact/, lib/db/) are implementation details never imported by components directly.

types.name: AgentId
types.description: Branded string type for agent identifiers. Created via asAgentId() helper. Nominal typing prevents accidental use of plain strings.

types.name: ProjectId
types.description: Branded string type for project identifiers. Created via asProjectId() helper.

types.name: SessionId
types.description: Branded string type for agent session identifiers. Created via asSessionId() helper.

types.name: ComponentId
types.description: Branded string type for PACT component identifiers. Created via asComponentId() helper.

types.name: ContractId
types.description: Branded string type for PACT contract identifiers. Created via asContractId() helper.

types.name: ISOTimestamp
types.description: ISO 8601 datetime string (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ss.sssZ). Validated by Zod regex on ingestion.

types.name: CentsAmount
types.description: Integer representing US cents. Always >= 0. Used for all cost/spend values to avoid floating-point precision issues.

types.name: DataErrorKind
types.description: Discriminant tag for the error branch of DataResult. Three kinds cover all expected filesystem/parse failure modes.

types.name: DataError

fields.name: path
fields.description: Filesystem path or resource identifier that caused the error.

fields.name: message
fields.description: Human-readable description of what went wrong.

fields.name: cause
fields.description: Optional underlying error message or Zod issue summary for debugging.

types.description: Structured error value returned in the error branch of DataResult. Carries enough context for logging and user-facing messages.

types.name: DataResultOk

fields.name: ok
fields.description: Always true for success branch. Discriminant field.

fields.name: data
fields.description: The typed payload. Generic parameter T in implementation — typed as the specific domain type at each call site.

types.description: Success branch of the DataResult discriminated union.

types.name: DataResultErr

fields.name: ok
fields.description: Always false for error branch. Discriminant field.

fields.name: error
fields.description: Structured error with kind, path, message, and optional cause.

types.description: Error branch of the DataResult discriminated union.

types.name: DataResult
types.description: Discriminated union representing either a successful typed value or a structured error. All public data-access functions return this. Discriminate on the 'ok' boolean field.

types.name: AgentStatus
types.description: Current operational status of an OpenClaw agent, derived from openclaw.json and agent process state.

types.name: AgentDefinition

fields.name: model
fields.description: LLM model identifier the agent is configured to use (e.g., 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514').

fields.name: workspace_path
fields.description: Absolute path to the agent's workspace directory.

fields.name: current_task
fields.description: Description or ID of the task the agent is currently working on, if any.

fields.name: last_active
fields.description: Timestamp of the agent's last recorded activity.

types.description: Parsed and validated agent definition from openclaw.json. Represents one agent in the team.

types.name: AgentList
types.description: List of all agent definitions.

types.name: PipelinePhase
types.description: PACT pipeline phases representing project progression. Ordered from earliest to latest.

types.name: ContractStatus
types.description: Status of a single PACT contract within a project.

types.name: TestStatus
types.description: Aggregate test status for a component or contract.

types.name: PactContract

fields.name: id
fields.description: Unique contract identifier derived from filename.

fields.name: component_id
fields.description: The component this contract belongs to.

fields.name: file_path
fields.description: Relative path to the contract file within the project.

fields.name: functions_count
fields.description: Number of functions defined in the contract.

fields.name: types_count
fields.description: Number of types defined in the contract.

types.description: A single PACT contract parsed from the contracts/ directory.

types.name: PactContractList
types.description: List of PACT contracts.

types.name: PactComponent

fields.name: parent_id
fields.description: Parent component ID for tree structure. Empty string for root.

fields.name: contract_ids
fields.description: List of contract IDs associated with this component.

fields.name: has_implementation
fields.description: Whether source files exist in src/ for this component.

fields.name: test_status
fields.description: Aggregate test status for this component.

types.description: A node in the PACT component tree, parsed from the decomposition/ directory.

types.name: PactComponentTree
types.description: Flat list of components forming a tree (parent_id references define structure).

types.name: ProjectDefinition

fields.name: root_path
fields.description: Absolute path to the project root directory.

fields.name: phase
fields.description: Current PACT pipeline phase, derived from directory contents.

fields.name: updated_at
fields.description: Last modification timestamp across all project files.

fields.name: components_count
fields.description: Total number of components in the decomposition.

fields.name: contracts_implemented
fields.description: Number of contracts with status IMPLEMENTED or higher.

fields.name: tests_passing
fields.description: Number of test suites currently passing.

types.description: Top-level project metadata parsed from pact.yaml in a PACT project directory.

types.name: ProjectList
types.description: List of all discovered projects.

types.name: ProjectPipeline

fields.name: project_id
fields.description: The project this pipeline belongs to.

types.description: Detailed pipeline state for a project, including component tree and all contracts.

types.name: PhaseProgress
types.description: Progress information for a single pipeline phase.

types.name: PhaseProgressMap
types.description: Progress for each pipeline phase. One entry per PipelinePhase variant.

types.name: TokenUsage

fields.name: cache_read_tokens
fields.description: Number of tokens served from cache (input).

fields.name: cache_write_tokens
fields.description: Number of tokens written to cache.

types.description: Token consumption for a single session or aggregation period.

types.name: SessionCost

fields.name: project_id
fields.description: Project the session was associated with, if any.

fields.name: model
fields.description: LLM model used in this session.

fields.name: ended_at
fields.description: Session end timestamp. Empty if session is still running.

fields.name: duration_seconds
fields.description: Session duration in seconds. 0 if still running.

types.description: Cost and token data for a single agent session, parsed from session JSON files.

types.name: SessionCostList
types.description: List of session cost records.

types.name: CostSummary

fields.name: total_cost_cents
fields.description: Sum of all session costs in cents.

fields.name: total_tokens
fields.description: Aggregated token usage across all sessions.

fields.name: session_count
fields.description: Number of sessions included in this summary.

fields.name: by_agent
fields.description: JSON-serialized map of AgentId -> CentsAmount. Serialized because contract types cannot express Map generics.

fields.name: by_model
fields.description: JSON-serialized map of model name -> CentsAmount.

types.description: Aggregated cost summary across multiple sessions, used for dashboard totals and charts.

types.name: CostFilter

fields.name: model
fields.description: Filter to a specific model name.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: Maximum number of results to return.

types.description: Filter criteria for querying session costs. All fields optional — omitted fields mean 'no filter'.

types.name: CostTimeSeriesPoint

fields.name: cost_cents
fields.description: Total cost in this time bucket.

fields.name: tokens
fields.description: Total tokens in this time bucket.

fields.name: session_count
fields.description: Number of sessions in this time bucket.

types.description: A single data point in a cost/token time series for charts.

types.name: CostTimeSeries
types.description: Time series of cost data points, ordered by timestamp ascending.

types.name: TimeGranularity
types.description: Time bucketing granularity for cost time series.

types.name: ActivityEvent

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: JSON-serialized additional metadata specific to the event type.

types.description: A single activity event for the activity feed, derived from agent session data and file changes.

types.name: ActivityEventType
types.description: Categories of activity events.

types.name: ActivityEventList
types.description: List of activity events, ordered by timestamp descending.

types.name: ActivityFilter

fields.name: event_types
fields.description: Filter to specific event types. Empty means all.

types.description: Filter criteria for querying activity events.

types.name: UserTask

validators.error_message: Title must be between 1 and 500 characters.

fields.name: assigned_agent_id
fields.description: Agent assigned to this task, if any.

types.description: A user-created task stored in SQLite. Dashboard-specific data not from OpenClaw/PACT.

types.name: UserTaskStatus
types.description: Status of a user-created task.

types.name: UserTaskList
types.description: List of user tasks.

types.name: UserTaskInput

validators.error_message: Title must be between 1 and 500 characters.

types.description: Input for creating or updating a user task. Validated with Zod before insertion.

types.name: DashboardOverview

fields.name: tasks_open
fields.description: Number of user tasks not DONE or CANCELLED.

fields.name: total_cost_today_cents
fields.description: Total cost for today in cents.

types.description: Aggregated overview data for the main dashboard page.

types.name: UserPreferences

validators.error_message: Refresh interval must be between 5 and 300 seconds.

fields.name: default_cost_granularity
fields.description: Default time granularity for cost charts.

fields.name: activity_feed_limit
fields.description: Number of activity events to show.

validators.error_message: Activity feed limit must be between 10 and 200.

types.description: User preferences stored in SQLite. Dashboard layout and display settings.

types.name: HealthStatus

fields.name: sqlite_ok
fields.description: Whether SQLite database is accessible and schema is current.

fields.name: openclaw_config_ok
fields.description: Whether openclaw.json is readable and valid.

fields.name: pact_directories_ok
fields.description: Whether configured PACT project directories are accessible.

fields.name: errors
fields.description: List of error messages for any failing checks.

types.description: Health check result for the data layer — used to verify filesystem paths and database connectivity.

functions.name: readJsonFile
functions.description: Low-level utility: reads a JSON file from disk, parses it, and validates against a Zod schema. Maps ENOENT → NOT_FOUND, JSON.parse/Zod errors → MALFORMED, other errors → IO_ERROR. Used internally by all JSON-based parsers. Generic over T (the Zod output type).

inputs.name: file_path
inputs.description: Absolute path to the JSON file to read.

inputs.name: schema_name
inputs.description: Name of the Zod schema to validate against (resolved internally from schema registry).

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: File does not exist at file_path (ENOENT)

error_cases.name: invalid_json
error_cases.condition: File exists but contains invalid JSON (SyntaxError)

error_cases.name: schema_validation_failed
error_cases.condition: File contains valid JSON but fails Zod schema validation

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: File exists but is not readable (EACCES)

error_cases.name: unexpected_fs_error
error_cases.condition: Any other filesystem error (disk failure, etc.)

preconditions: schema_name references a registered Zod schema
postconditions: If ok=true, data has been validated against the named Zod schema
If ok=false and kind=NOT_FOUND, the file does not exist at file_path
If ok=false and kind=MALFORMED, the file exists but contains invalid JSON or fails Zod validation
If ok=false and kind=IO_ERROR, an unexpected filesystem error occurred

structured_side_effects.target: file_path parameter — arbitrary JSON file on local filesystem
structured_side_effects.description: Reads and parses a single JSON file.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is file size

functions.name: readYamlFile
functions.description: Low-level utility: reads a YAML file from disk, parses it with js-yaml, and validates against a Zod schema. Same error mapping as readJsonFile. Used internally by PACT parsers for pact.yaml.

inputs.name: file_path
inputs.description: Absolute path to the YAML file to read.

inputs.name: schema_name
inputs.description: Name of the Zod schema to validate against.

error_cases.name: file_not_found
error_cases.condition: File does not exist (ENOENT)

error_cases.name: invalid_yaml
error_cases.condition: File contains invalid YAML syntax

error_cases.name: schema_validation_failed
error_cases.condition: YAML is valid but fails Zod schema

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: Not readable (EACCES)

error_cases.name: unexpected_fs_error
error_cases.condition: Other filesystem error

preconditions: schema_name references a registered Zod schema
postconditions: If ok=true, data has been validated against the named Zod schema
If ok=false and kind=NOT_FOUND, the file does not exist
If ok=false and kind=MALFORMED, the file exists but fails YAML parse or Zod validation
If ok=false and kind=IO_ERROR, an unexpected filesystem error occurred

structured_side_effects.target: file_path parameter — arbitrary YAML file on local filesystem
structured_side_effects.description: Reads and parses a single YAML file.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is file size

functions.name: getAgents
functions.description: Returns all agent definitions from openclaw.json. Reads and parses the OpenClaw configuration file, validates with Zod, and returns typed AgentDefinition list. If the file is missing or malformed, returns appropriate DataError.

error_cases.name: config_not_found
error_cases.condition: openclaw.json does not exist at the configured path

error_cases.name: config_malformed
error_cases.condition: openclaw.json exists but fails Zod validation

error_cases.name: config_io_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected error reading openclaw.json

preconditions: OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH environment variable or default path is configured
postconditions: If ok=true, data is a valid AgentList with all agents from openclaw.json
Each agent in the list has a unique id
Agent status values are derived from current process state where detectable

structured_side_effects.description: Reads the OpenClaw agent configuration file from the configured path.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of agents

functions.name: getAgent
functions.description: Returns a single agent definition by ID. Reads openclaw.json, finds the matching agent, and returns it. Returns NOT_FOUND if the agent ID doesn't exist in the config.

inputs.name: agent_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the agent to retrieve.

error_cases.name: agent_not_found
error_cases.condition: No agent with the given ID exists in openclaw.json

error_cases.name: config_not_found
error_cases.condition: openclaw.json does not exist

error_cases.name: config_malformed
error_cases.condition: openclaw.json fails validation

error_cases.name: config_io_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected read error

preconditions: agent_id is a non-empty branded AgentId string
postconditions: If ok=true, data is a single AgentDefinition with id matching agent_id
If ok=false and kind=NOT_FOUND, no agent with that ID exists in openclaw.json

structured_side_effects.description: Reads OpenClaw config to find the requested agent.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of agents

functions.name: getProjects
functions.description: Discovers and returns all PACT projects from configured project directories. Scans each directory for pact.yaml, parses project metadata, derives pipeline phase from directory structure, and aggregates component/contract/test counts.

error_cases.name: projects_dir_not_found
error_cases.condition: Configured projects directory does not exist

error_cases.name: projects_dir_io_error
error_cases.condition: Cannot read the projects directory

error_cases.name: partial_parse_failures
error_cases.condition: Some project directories have malformed pact.yaml — those are skipped and included in the error cause but function still returns ok=true with valid projects

preconditions: PACT_PROJECTS_DIR environment variable or default path is configured
postconditions: If ok=true, data is a ProjectList with one entry per discovered pact.yaml
Each project has accurate counts derived from scanning its directory structure
Projects are sorted by updated_at descending (most recently modified first)

structured_side_effects.target: PACT project directories — scans for pact.yaml, decomposition/, contracts/, tests/, src/
structured_side_effects.description: Reads multiple directories and files to discover and parse project metadata.

performance_budget.complexity: O(p * c) where p is number of projects and c is average components per project

functions.name: getProject
functions.description: Returns a single project definition by ID. Scans configured project directories for a matching pact.yaml, parses and returns the project metadata.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: The unique identifier of the project to retrieve.

error_cases.name: project_not_found
error_cases.condition: No project with the given ID exists in any configured directory

error_cases.name: project_malformed
error_cases.condition: Project directory exists but pact.yaml fails validation

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected filesystem error during scan

preconditions: project_id is a non-empty branded ProjectId string
postconditions: If ok=true, data is a single ProjectDefinition with id matching project_id
Counts (components, contracts, tests) are accurate at time of read

structured_side_effects.target: PACT project directory for the given project_id
structured_side_effects.description: Reads pact.yaml and scans directory structure for the requested project.

performance_budget.complexity: O(c) where c is number of components in the project

functions.name: getProjectPipeline
functions.description: Returns the full pipeline state for a project, including the complete component tree, all contracts, and phase-by-phase progress. This is the detailed view used by the project detail page.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: The project to get pipeline data for.

error_cases.name: project_not_found
error_cases.condition: No project with the given ID exists

error_cases.name: decomposition_malformed
error_cases.condition: decomposition/ directory exists but contains invalid structure

error_cases.name: contracts_malformed
error_cases.condition: contracts/ directory exists but contains unparseable contract files

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected filesystem error

preconditions: project_id is a non-empty branded ProjectId string
postconditions: If ok=true, data is a ProjectPipeline with complete component tree and contract list
Component parent_id references form a valid tree (no cycles, all referenced IDs exist)
Phase progress percentages are consistent with component/contract counts
Contracts are linked to valid component IDs within the same project

structured_side_effects.target: Project directory: pact.yaml, decomposition/, contracts/, tests/, src/
structured_side_effects.description: Deep scan of an entire PACT project directory structure.

performance_budget.complexity: O(c + k) where c is components and k is contracts

functions.name: getSessionCosts
functions.description: Returns a filtered, paginated list of session cost records. Reads agent session JSON files from agent workspace directories and returns parsed cost/token data. Supports filtering by agent, project, model, and date range.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Filter and pagination criteria for session costs.

error_cases.name: sessions_dir_not_found
error_cases.condition: No session directories found (agents may not have run yet)

error_cases.name: malformed_sessions_skipped
error_cases.condition: Some session files are malformed — skipped, valid sessions still returned

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected filesystem error reading session files

postconditions: If ok=true, data is a SessionCostList matching the filter criteria
Results are ordered by started_at descending
Result count <= filter.limit
All cost_cents values are non-negative integers

structured_side_effects.description: Reads session cost data from agent workspace directories.

performance_budget.complexity: O(s * log s) where s is total number of session files (sort + filter)

functions.name: getCostSummary
functions.description: Returns an aggregated cost summary across sessions matching the given filter. Computes totals by agent and model. Used for dashboard overview and cost reporting.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Filter criteria for which sessions to include in the summary.

error_cases.name: no_sessions
error_cases.condition: No sessions match the filter (returns ok with zero totals, not an error)

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected error reading session data

postconditions: If ok=true, data is a CostSummary with accurate aggregations
total_cost_cents equals the sum of all matching session cost_cents
session_count equals the number of sessions included
by_agent and by_model breakdowns sum to total_cost_cents

structured_side_effects.description: Reads all matching session files for aggregation.

performance_budget.complexity: O(s) where s is number of matching sessions

functions.name: getCostTimeSeries
functions.description: Returns time-bucketed cost data for chart rendering. Groups session costs into time buckets of the specified granularity.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Filter criteria for which sessions to include.

inputs.name: granularity
inputs.description: Time bucket size (HOUR, DAY, or WEEK).

error_cases.name: no_data
error_cases.condition: No session data exists for the given period

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected error reading session data

preconditions: filter.from_date and filter.to_date should be set for meaningful results
postconditions: If ok=true, data is a CostTimeSeries ordered by timestamp ascending
Each point represents one time bucket with aggregated cost and token data
No gaps — empty buckets are included with zero values

structured_side_effects.description: Reads session files and buckets them by time.

performance_budget.complexity: O(s + b) where s is sessions and b is number of time buckets

functions.name: getActivityEvents
functions.description: Returns a filtered, paginated list of activity events for the activity feed. Derives events from agent session data, file modification times, and contract/test changes. Results are merged and sorted by timestamp.

inputs.name: filter
inputs.description: Filter and pagination criteria for activity events.

error_cases.name: no_data_sources
error_cases.condition: No agent workspaces or project directories found

error_cases.name: partial_read_failures
error_cases.condition: Some data sources are unreadable — events from valid sources still returned

error_cases.name: io_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected filesystem error

postconditions: If ok=true, data is an ActivityEventList ordered by timestamp descending
Result count <= filter.limit
Each event has a unique id

structured_side_effects.target: Agent workspaces and PACT project directories
structured_side_effects.description: Reads multiple data sources to derive activity events.

performance_budget.complexity: O(e * log e) where e is total events before filtering

functions.name: getTasks
functions.description: Returns all user tasks from SQLite, optionally filtered by status, agent, or project. Dashboard-specific data stored in SQLite, not from OpenClaw/PACT files.

inputs.name: status
inputs.description: Filter by UserTaskStatus variant name. Empty string means all statuses.

inputs.name: assigned_agent_id
inputs.description: Filter by assigned agent. Empty string means all.

inputs.name: project_id
inputs.description: Filter by project. Empty string means all.

inputs.name: limit
inputs.description: Maximum results.

inputs.name: offset
inputs.description: Pagination offset.

error_cases.name: db_not_initialized
error_cases.condition: SQLite database file does not exist or tasks table is missing

error_cases.name: invalid_status_filter
error_cases.condition: Status parameter is not a valid UserTaskStatus variant

error_cases.name: db_query_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected SQLite error during query

preconditions: SQLite database is initialized with tasks table
postconditions: If ok=true, data is a UserTaskList matching filter criteria
Results ordered by priority ascending then created_at descending
Result count <= limit

structured_side_effects.description: Executes SELECT query on the tasks table.

performance_budget.complexity: O(n) where n is number of matching tasks

functions.name: getTask
functions.description: Returns a single user task by its UUID from SQLite.

inputs.name: task_id
inputs.description: UUID of the task to retrieve.

error_cases.name: task_not_found
error_cases.condition: No task with the given UUID exists

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: Unexpected SQLite error

postconditions: If ok=true, data is a single UserTask with matching id

structured_side_effects.description: Executes SELECT query for a single task.

performance_budget.complexity: O(1) — primary key lookup

functions.name: createTask
functions.description: Creates a new user task in SQLite. Validates input with Zod, generates a UUID, sets timestamps, and inserts the row. Returns the complete created task.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Task creation data, validated with Zod.

error_cases.name: validation_failed
error_cases.condition: Input fails Zod validation (title too long, invalid priority, etc.)

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite INSERT fails

postconditions: If ok=true, data is the created UserTask with generated id and timestamps
The task exists in SQLite and can be retrieved by getTask(id)
created_at and updated_at are set to the current time

structured_side_effects.description: Inserts a new row into the tasks table.

functions.name: updateTask
functions.description: Updates an existing user task in SQLite. Validates input with Zod, updates only provided fields, bumps updated_at. Returns the complete updated task.

inputs.name: task_id
inputs.description: UUID of the task to update.

inputs.name: input
inputs.description: Fields to update. All fields are applied (use current values for unchanged fields).

error_cases.name: task_not_found
error_cases.condition: No task with the given UUID exists

error_cases.name: validation_failed
error_cases.condition: Input fails Zod validation

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite UPDATE fails

postconditions: If ok=true, data is the updated UserTask with bumped updated_at
The task reflects all values from input

structured_side_effects.description: Updates an existing row in the tasks table.

functions.name: deleteTask
functions.description: Deletes a user task from SQLite by UUID. Returns the deleted task for confirmation, or NOT_FOUND if it doesn't exist.

inputs.name: task_id
inputs.description: UUID of the task to delete.

error_cases.name: task_not_found
error_cases.condition: No task with the given UUID exists

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite DELETE fails

postconditions: If ok=true, data is the deleted UserTask (snapshot before deletion)
The task no longer exists in SQLite after successful deletion

structured_side_effects.description: Deletes a row from the tasks table.

functions.name: getDashboardOverview
functions.description: Returns aggregated overview data for the main dashboard page. Combines agent count/status from openclaw.json, project count from PACT directories, open task count from SQLite, today's cost totals from session files, and recent activity. Gracefully handles partial failures — if agents are unavailable, returns 0 for agent counts but still provides project/task data.

error_cases.name: all_sources_failed
error_cases.condition: Every data source (openclaw, pact dirs, sqlite) is unavailable

error_cases.name: partial_failure
error_cases.condition: Some sources unavailable — returns ok=true with zero values for failed sources and populated error cause describing what failed

postconditions: If ok=true, data is a DashboardOverview with best-effort data from all sources
Counts are non-negative integers
recent_activity contains at most 10 events

structured_side_effects.target: openclaw.json, PACT project directories, agent session files
structured_side_effects.description: Reads from all filesystem data sources.
Queries task counts from SQLite.

performance_budget.complexity: O(a + p*c + s) where a=agents, p=projects, c=avg components, s=today's sessions

functions.name: getPreferences
functions.description: Returns user preferences from SQLite. If no preferences row exists, returns defaults. Since this is a single-user dashboard, there is only one preferences record.

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite query fails

postconditions: If ok=true, data is a UserPreferences struct (never NOT_FOUND — defaults are returned)

structured_side_effects.description: Reads preferences from the preferences table.

functions.name: updatePreferences
functions.description: Updates user preferences in SQLite. Upserts the single preferences row. Validates input with Zod.

inputs.name: preferences
inputs.description: Complete preferences to save (full replace, not partial update).

error_cases.name: validation_failed
error_cases.condition: Preferences fail Zod validation (e.g., refresh_interval out of range)

error_cases.name: db_error
error_cases.condition: SQLite UPSERT fails

postconditions: If ok=true, data is the saved UserPreferences
Subsequent getPreferences() returns these values

structured_side_effects.description: Upserts the single preferences row.

functions.name: initializeDatabase
functions.description: Creates the SQLite database file (if it doesn't exist) and runs schema migrations to ensure all tables (tasks, preferences) are present and up-to-date. Called once at application startup. Safe to call multiple times (idempotent via IF NOT EXISTS).

inputs.name: db_path
inputs.description: Path to the SQLite database file. Defaults to 'dashboard.db' in the project root.

error_cases.name: permission_denied
error_cases.condition: Cannot create or write to the database file

error_cases.name: migration_failed
error_cases.condition: Schema migration SQL fails (e.g., incompatible schema change)

error_cases.name: corrupt_database
error_cases.condition: Database file exists but is corrupted

preconditions: db_path parent directory exists and is writable
postconditions: If ok=true, SQLite database exists with all required tables and indexes
Schema version is stored and can be checked for future migrations
Existing data is preserved across schema migrations

structured_side_effects.description: Creates or migrates the SQLite database file.

structured_side_effects.target: SQLite schema — CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS, migrations
structured_side_effects.description: Ensures all required tables and indexes exist.

performance_budget.complexity: O(m) where m is number of pending migrations

functions.name: healthCheck
functions.description: Performs a health check on all data sources: verifies SQLite connectivity, openclaw.json readability, and PACT directory accessibility. Returns a structured HealthStatus with individual check results.
postconditions: Always returns ok=true with a HealthStatus (health check itself never fails with DataError)
Individual check fields (sqlite_ok, openclaw_config_ok, pact_directories_ok) reflect actual status
errors list contains details for any failing checks

structured_side_effects.description: Performs lightweight read checks on all data sources.

performance_budget.complexity: O(1) — fixed number of checks

functions.name: asAgentId
functions.description: Branded type constructor: validates and brands a plain string as an AgentId. Pure function, no I/O.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Plain string to brand as AgentId.

validators.error_message: Agent ID must be between 1 and 255 characters.

error_cases.name: empty_id
error_cases.condition: value is an empty string

postconditions: Returned value is a branded AgentId that can be used where AgentId is required

functions.name: asProjectId
functions.description: Branded type constructor: validates and brands a plain string as a ProjectId. Pure function, no I/O.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Plain string to brand as ProjectId.

validators.error_message: Project ID must be between 1 and 255 characters.

error_cases.name: empty_id
error_cases.condition: value is an empty string

postconditions: Returned value is a branded ProjectId

functions.description: Branded type constructor: validates and brands a plain string as a SessionId. Pure function, no I/O.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Plain string to brand as SessionId.

validators.error_message: Session ID must be between 1 and 255 characters.

error_cases.name: empty_id
error_cases.condition: value is an empty string

postconditions: Returned value is a branded SessionId

functions.description: Branded type constructor: validates and brands a plain string as a ComponentId. Pure function, no I/O.

inputs.name: value
inputs.description: Plain string to brand as ComponentId.

validators.error_message: Component ID must be between 1 and 255 characters.

error_cases.name: empty_id
error_cases.condition: value is an empty string

postconditions: Returned value is a branded ComponentId

functions.name: isOk
functions.description: Type guard / discriminator: narrows a DataResult to DataResultOk. Pure function used for type-safe result handling. Returns true if result.ok === true.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: The DataResult to check.

postconditions: Returns true if result.ok === true, false otherwise
When true, result can be safely narrowed to DataResultOk in TypeScript

functions.name: isErr
functions.description: Type guard / discriminator: narrows a DataResult to DataResultErr. Pure function. Returns true if result.ok === false.

inputs.name: result
inputs.description: The DataResult to check.

postconditions: Returns true if result.ok === false, false otherwise
When true, result can be safely narrowed to DataResultErr in TypeScript

functions.name: makeOk
functions.description: Constructor: creates a DataResultOk from a typed data value. Pure function used internally and in tests.

inputs.name: data
inputs.description: The success payload of type T.

postconditions: Returned result has ok=true and data set to the input

functions.name: makeErr
functions.description: Constructor: creates a DataResultErr from a DataError. Pure function used internally and in tests.

inputs.name: kind
inputs.description: The error category.

inputs.name: path
inputs.description: The file path or resource that caused the error.

inputs.name: message
inputs.description: Human-readable error description.

inputs.name: cause
inputs.description: Optional underlying error details.

postconditions: Returned result has ok=false and error populated with the given values

invariants: All public functions return DataResult<T> — never throw for expected failures (NOT_FOUND, MALFORMED, IO_ERROR)
Components and API routes never import from lib/openclaw/, lib/pact/, or lib/db/ directly — only from lib/data/
All external file data is validated through Zod schemas using .safeParse(); raw unvalidated data never escapes the module boundary
Domain IDs (AgentId, ProjectId, SessionId, ComponentId, ContractId) are branded string types — plain strings cannot be passed where branded types are expected
Costs are always represented as integer cents (never floating-point dollars)
readJsonFile and readYamlFile always map ENOENT to NOT_FOUND, Zod/parse errors to MALFORMED, and all other errors to IO_ERROR
SQLite database is opened synchronously via better-sqlite3 and the connection is reused (singleton pattern)
No function in this module performs network I/O — all reads are from local filesystem or local SQLite
Fixture-based tests use real files on disk — no mocking of fs module
